etymology.description: Quadruplication of 一(壹) -> four horizontal lines -> 四(四)
etymology.interpretation: The character 亖(sǐ, "four lines") is an ideogram representing the number four through four parallel horizontal lines. It was used in ancient Chinese writing before being replaced by 四 (sì, "four") to avoid confusion with 二 (èr, "two") and 三 (sān, "three") in vertical text.
